---
title: "Working Papers in Critical Search"
pagetitle: "Working Papers in Critical Search"
description: "A home for history that shows its work."
format:
  html:
    page-layout: custom
---

```{=html}
<section class="wpcs-wrap wpcs-masthead">
  <div class="wpcs-masthead__grid">
    <div class="wpcs-micro">Est. 2026<br>Working papers</div>
    <div>
      <h1 class="wpcs-hero-h1">A home for history that <em>shows its work.</em></h1>
      <p class="wpcs-lead">
        A flexible venue for computational history — draft papers, dataset notes,
        method sketches, and short pieces that don't fit traditional formats.
        Code included, methods documented, limitations acknowledged.
      </p>
      <div class="wpcs-masthead__ctas">
        <a href="https://working-papers-in-critical-search.github.io/home/papers/" class="wpcs-mono wpcs-cta wpcs-cta--primary">Read the papers →</a>
        <a href="https://working-papers-in-critical-search.github.io/home/submit.html" class="wpcs-mono wpcs-cta">Submit your work →</a>
      </div>
    </div>
    <div class="wpcs-masthead__vlabel">Paper count: 1</div>
  </div>
</section>

<section class="wpcs-wrap wpcs-section">
  <div class="wpcs-section-head">
    <div class="wpcs-micro">№ 01</div>
    <h2>What we publish</h2>
    <div class="wpcs-section-head__trailing">4 formats</div>
  </div>
  <div class="wpcs-row">
    <div></div>
    <div class="wpcs-formats">
      <div class="wpcs-format-card">
        <div class="wpcs-micro wpcs-format-card__idx">01</div>
        <h3>Draft papers</h3>
        <p>Drafts on their way to traditional journals — get citations and feedback while you revise.</p>
      </div>
      <div class="wpcs-format-card">
        <div class="wpcs-micro wpcs-format-card__idx">02</div>
        <h3>Dataset papers</h3>
        <p>Documentation for the historical data you've created, with confidence reports and known limitations.</p>
      </div>
      <div class="wpcs-format-card">
        <div class="wpcs-micro wpcs-format-card__idx">03</div>
        <h3>Method notes</h3>
        <p>Tools, pipelines, techniques — what works, what doesn't, with caveats.</p>
      </div>
      <div class="wpcs-format-card">
        <div class="wpcs-micro wpcs-format-card__idx">04</div>
        <h3>Short pieces</h3>
        <p>Findings and arguments that don't fit the 8,000-word monograph shape.</p>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>
</section>

<section class="wpcs-band">
  <div class="wpcs-wrap">
    <div class="wpcs-section-head">
      <div class="wpcs-micro">№ 02</div>
      <h2>Latest papers</h2>
      <div class="wpcs-section-head__trailing">Launch papers forthcoming</div>
    </div>
    <div class="wpcs-row">
      <div></div>
      <div class="wpcs-empty">
        <p class="wpcs-lead" style="max-width: 680px;">
          The first paper — <em>Building Working Papers in Critical Search</em> by the editors —
          publishes at launch. Two further papers follow within a fortnight.
        </p>
        <p class="wpcs-small-mono" style="margin-top: 20px;">
          <a href="https://working-papers-in-critical-search.github.io/home/papers/" style="border-bottom: 1px solid var(--ink, #1a1a2e);">All papers →</a>
        </p>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>
</section>

<section class="wpcs-wrap wpcs-section">
  <div class="wpcs-section-head">
    <div class="wpcs-micro">№ 03</div>
    <h2>How to cite</h2>
    <div class="wpcs-section-head__trailing">DOI via Zenodo</div>
  </div>
  <div class="wpcs-row">
    <div class="wpcs-small-mono" style="line-height: 1.7;">
      Every paper gets a permanent DOI. Cite the specific version you used — we keep all versions citeable.
    </div>
    <div>
      <pre class="wpcs-cite-block">Author (Year). "Title." Working Papers in Critical
  Search, Paper NNN, vX.X.
DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.XXXXXXX</pre>
      <p class="wpcs-cite-note">
        BibTeX and RIS exports are available on each paper's page. Versions are frozen for
        citation on request; we also mint DOIs for significant revisions.
      </p>
    </div>
  </div>
</section>

<section class="wpcs-wrap wpcs-section">
  <div class="wpcs-section-head">
    <div class="wpcs-micro">№ 04</div>
    <h2>Editors</h2>
    <div class="wpcs-section-head__trailing">Two, for now</div>
  </div>
  <div class="wpcs-row">
    <div></div>
    <div class="wpcs-editors">
      <div class="wpcs-editor">
        <div class="wpcs-micro">Co-editor</div>
        <h3><a href="https://www.joguldi.com/">Jo Guldi</a></h3>
        <div class="wpcs-small-mono">Emory University</div>
        <p>
          Historian of modern Britain and the long history of land. Works on text mining of
          parliamentary debates, historiography of the longue durée, and the ethics of
          computational history.
        </p>
        <div class="wpcs-editor__tags">
          <span class="wpcs-tag">19c Britain</span>
          <span class="wpcs-tag">text mining</span>
          <span class="wpcs-tag">land history</span>
        </div>
      </div>
      <div class="wpcs-editor">
        <div class="wpcs-micro">Co-editor</div>
        <h3><a href="https://jburnford.github.io/">Jim Clifford</a></h3>
        <div class="wpcs-small-mono">University of Saskatchewan</div>
        <p>
          Environmental historian working on commodity chains, GIS, and historical OCR.
          Co-directs the Sustainability and the Hinterland project; builds tools for
          historians who want to work with large, messy sources.
        </p>
        <div class="wpcs-editor__tags">
          <span class="wpcs-tag">environmental history</span>
          <span class="wpcs-tag">GIS</span>
          <span class="wpcs-tag">Canadian history</span>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>
</section>

<section class="wpcs-wrap wpcs-section wpcs-section--flush">
  <div class="wpcs-submit-cta">
    <div class="wpcs-micro">Note</div>
    <h3>We welcome submissions from historians using computational methods.</h3>
    <a href="https://working-papers-in-critical-search.github.io/home/submit.html" class="wpcs-mono wpcs-submit-cta__btn">Submit →</a>
  </div>
</section>
```
